summary: Provision new Elastic Beanstalk applications. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — environments and day-2 operations land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new Elastic Beanstalk application
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Elastic Beanstalk application; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new application as a complete specification: name, description, and an optional application-version retention rule. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: app_name
params.label: Application name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like checkout-web. Names the application in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Shown in the Elastic Beanstalk console's application list.

params.name: lifecycle_service_role
params.label: Version-lifecycle service role
params.help: Required only if you set a version-retention rule below; leave both blank to keep every application version forever.

params.name: max_age_in_days
params.label: Delete versions older than (days)
params.help: Application versions older than this are deleted automatically.

params.name: delete_source_from_s3
params.label: Delete source bundle from S3 too
params.help: Also removes the version's source bundle from the S3 storage bucket when it is deleted.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this application does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this application.

decisions: Environments (the running application stack) are provisioned as an engineer follow-up after the application exists
Version retention by COUNT instead of age (max_count) is an engineer decision — this form offers the age-based rule
